channel.title: Latest Finextra Research Wealth management Headlines
channel.description: The Latest Financial IT Wealth management Headlines

item.title: Turkish investment app Midas raises $80m
item.description: Istanbul-based retail investing startup Midas has raised $80 million in Series B funding led by QED Investors.

item.title: Robinhood UK rolls out AI investing assistant to decode market movements
item.description: Robinhood is rolling out an AI-powered investing assistant to help UK users interpret and navigate market movements.

item.title: Bullish shares more than double in NYSE debut
item.description: Investor confidence in the cryptocurrency sector was punctuated after Bullish was valued at $13.16 billion last week, setting the pace for other digital asset firms looking to list in the US.

item.title: UK retail investors favour index trackers - Investment Association
item.description: Investment Association (IA) data reveals that index trackers attracted £1.1 billion in inflows in June 2025 marking a strong continuation of momentum for passive investing, particularly in equity exposures across Europe and North America.

item.title: Cinven makes majority investment in Objectway
item.description: Private equity firm Cinven has made a majority investment in Italian wealth management technology provider ObjectWay. Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

item.title: Spend management startup Stavtar secures $55m funding boost
item.description: Stavtar Solutions, a US-based startup with a SaaS platform for expense allocations and spend management, has completed a series A funding round worth $55m

item.title: Asset manager associations join international blockchain initiative
item.description: The respective trade associations for the UK and Singapore's asset management industries have signed up to Project Guardian, a cross-border initiative led by the Monetary Authority of Singapore and designed to promote use-cases for blockchain.

item.title: Brazil's QI Tech hunts acquisitions after $63m raise
item.description: Brazilian financial infrastructure fintech QI Tech has raised $63 million in a Series B extension funding led by General Atlantic.

item.title: No AI? No exit, says investment bank Artis Partners
item.description: Fintechs that fail to embed AI into their operating model will find it increasingly difficult to orchestrate an exit, according to investment bank and tech advisory firm Artis Partners.

item.title: SS&C acquires funds network Calastone in $1bn deal
item.description: Investment funds processing network Calastone has been bought by US-based funds servicing firm and software vendor SS&C Technologies in a deal worth £776m ($1.03bn).

item.title: Anthony Thomson to launch new global wealth bank
item.description: Anthony Thomson, the founder of Metro Bank and Atom Bank, is seeking to shake up the private banking market with the creation of a new international bank to serve family offices and ultra high-net-worth individuals.

item.title: Lightyear eyes AI opportunity on $28 million fund raise
item.description: Investment firm Lightyear has closed a $23 million Series B funding round as it eyes opportunities to bring artificial intelligence to it platform.

item.title: FCA approves first Pisces operator
item.description: On 21 August, the FCA approved the London Stock Exchange to operate a PISCES platform, a new type of private stock market.

item.title: Wells Fargo adds alternative investments to Unified Managed Account programme
item.description: Wells Fargo’s Wealth & Investment Management (WIM) division, in collaboration with InvestCloud, now offers alternative investments as an eligible investment in its Personalized Unified Managed Account (Personalized UMA) program.

item.title: DriveWealth names Naureen Hassan as CEO
item.description: DriveWealth, a leading financial technology platform providing Brokerage-as-a-Service, has announced the appointment of Naureen Hassan as Global Chief Executive Officer (CEO).

item.title: Openbank migrates investment accounts to Upvest
item.description: Openbank, part of the Santander Group, has chosen Upvest as its new investment infrastructure provider.

item.title: FNZ rolls out Advisor AI
item.description: FNZ, the global wealth management platform, has today announced the global launch of FNZ Advisor AI, a generative AI solution embedded directly into FNZ’s market leading wealth management platform.

item.title: Susanne Krehl joins wealthAPI as chief growth officer
item.description: wealthAPI GmbH, a leading German provider of wealth management interfaces, is appointing Susanne Krehl as Chief Growth Officer (CGO), effective September 1st, 2025. As part of the management team alongside founders André Rabenstein and Wolfram Stacklies, Krehl will focus on the growth and European expansion of the Berlin-based WealthTech company.

item.title: InvestiFi names Patrick McNally chief product officer
item.description: InvestiFi, the leading InvestTech platform for banks and credit unions, that enables investing to and from deposit accounts, has today announced Patrick McNally as its new Chief Product Officer.

item.title: Broadridge invests in AI platform Uptiq
item.description: Broadridge Financial Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: BR), a global Fintech leader, has announced a new strategic partnership and minority investment in Uptiq, an AI platform for financial services.

item.title: InvestCloud taps AI to drive advisor productivity
item.description: InvestCloud, a global leader in wealth technology, today announced the first of a new generation of AI-enabled solutions to help advisors increase productivity and deliver greater value to clients.

item.title: Seccl appoints Jonathan Dees as its new UK CEO, as David Ferguson transitions to executive chair
item.description: Seccl, the Octopus-owned embedded investment platform, has appointed Jonathan Dees as its new UK CEO, subject to regulatory approval.

item.title: BUX introduces actively managed multi-asset ETF Plans to retail investors
item.description: BUX Prime Investment Plans have officially launched, offering a first-of-its-kind solution in Europe.

item.title: South Africa's Luno lists 44 tokenised blue-chip US stocks
item.description: South Africa's leading crypto platform Luno has launched tokenised stocks and ETFs, offering South Africans easy access to 61 of the world's most valuable US-listed companies and indices (44 available today, with the remainder launching shortly).

item.title: How Technology Has Democratised The Wealth Management Sector
item.description: Speaking with FinextraTV at the Communify Insights Experience, Andrew Bresler, CEO, Saxo shared his opinion on how wealth management is experiencing evolving transformation. Discussing how technology has helped to democratise wealth and provide more inclusivity, Bresler also describes AI as a company's exoskeleton: everything they can do but faster, better and more intelligent.

item.title: How Key Trends Are Redefining Wealth Management in 2025
item.description: Attending the Communify Insights Experience in London, Rupert Baron, Former CEO, Investment Management, Rathbones joined the FinextraTV studio and gave his perspective on what 'wealth' actually means in an industry of growing complexity. Defining wealth as a merge of investment and financial planning, Barron provided overviews on consolidation, private equity and AI, what it is that has driven such change, and how firms can move forward in the modern era.

item.title: How to Drive Engagement and Clarity Around Investment Decision-Making
item.description: Speaking to FinextraTV at Communify Insights Experience in London, Holly Mackay, Founder and MD, Boring Money explained what a 'bad' wealth decision is and how the industry can become more engaging. From storytelling to AI, Mackay shares her what she hopes will evolve within the industry across the next few years.

item.title: How to Turn Data Into Insight in a New Era of Financial Intelligence
item.description: Stepping away from a busy event, Leon Saunders Calvert, President & Managing Director - B2B, The Economist Group sat down with FinextraTV to discuss the role of data in a new era of financial intelligence. Describing how bigger data isn't as good as qualified data, Sanders Calvert helps to contextualise some of the key considerations when interoperating data in the current industry landscape.

item.title: Do We Need More Client Solutions and Less Products?
item.description: Providing insights into where the future of investment technology should be headed, Bernard del Rey, founder and CEO, Capital Preferences described how the industry has typically been too invested in products and portfolios. What he believes should be the central focus for the future now are solutions that provide a better, clearer understanding of the end client in order to inform hyperpersonalization.

item.title: Wealth Management: Both Easier & More Complex Than It Ever Was
item.description: Attending the Communify Fincentric Experience 2025, Michael Smith, EVP - Business Development, Communify Fincentric spoke to FinextraTV about the seemingly conflicting outlook of the current wealth management industry. In some sense, Smith believes, there are more tools to make the process easier but, at the same time, it is harder than ever to be successful. Diving into client requirements and the definition of a good product, Smith provides his expert analysis of the situation.

item.title: The Importance of Events: Trust, Partnership & Education
item.description: Joining the FinextraTV studio at the Communify Fincentric Experience, Michael Rodgers, EVP - Events, Communify Fincentric explains the benefits of an industry event and how they provide a strong, trustworthy space to connect with clients and suppliers alike, as well as informing, education and inspiring. Using AI as an example of the overwhelming market of information, Rodgers describes how events have a special way of cutting through that noise and complexity.

item.title: How Digital Assets Are Leading Wealth Portfolios
item.description: Joining FinextraTV at Money 20/20 in Amsterdam, Waqar Chaudry, Head of Digital Assets, Financing and Securities Services, Standard Chartered explains the long-term outlook on digital assets in wealth portfolios. Providing examples of how cryptocurrencies, especially, have outperformed other asset classes, Chaudry describes how corporates, specifically, have become key drivers of this popularity. As part of this, he further explains how more traditional institutions can provide unique insights to this more modern innovation.

item.title: The 3 Most Compelling AI Use Cases in Wealth Management
item.description: As part of our new Future of Wealth Series, Joe Bonanno, Global Head of Data Analytics & Innovation - Citi Wealth, Citi joined FinextraTV to discuss some of the key components of the current wealth management sector. Specifically, Bonanno brings insights into how AI is being receieved across the industry and the ways in which it is particularly useful in relation to data inputs for large language models, task optimisation and research.

item.title: How AI Can Reduce Decision Fatigue for Asset Managers
item.description: Joining the FinextraTV Virtual Studio, Phil Cox, COO, Russell Investments and Roshan Shetty, BFSI & Public Sector Head - Americas, Tech Mahindra discuss the decision fatigue within asset management currently and how AI helps alleviate that. Splitting the task abilities that can be improved into analysis, portfolio construction, risk management and trading strategies, Shetty and Cox debate the ease if AI adoption into existing processes and how information-based tools have risen to the top.

item.title: AI & Wealth: How to Build Trust Through Impact & Personalisation
item.description: Whilst at Money 20/20 in Amsterdam, Bahadir Yilmaz, Chief Analytics Officer, ING joined FinextraTV to provide some data insights in relation to growing AI conversations. Describing the challenge of taking data from its origins into actionable, presentable insights for clients, Yilmaz explains how a succesful, easy process will help to increase client's comfort with sharing larger, useful portions of data. Yilmaz, while speaking to AI use cases, says that clients are not interested in what happens behind-the-scenes, they care more for faster services and experiences where they can personally notice the change.

item.title: Australian Wealth: How Superannuation Growth is Prompting Technology Conversations
item.description: Whilst at the Communify Fincentric Experience 2025, Lachlan Hardy, Head of Sales & Business Development - APAC, Communify Fincentric helped to provide his unique insight into how the Australian wealth market compares to others. Speaking to the growth of self-directed wealth and superannuation funds, Hardy describes how one of the key considerations are around how technology is interacting and enabling this growth.

item.title: How The Future of Data is Personalisation & Scalability
item.description: Stepping away from a busy Communify Fincentric User Experience 2025, Nicole Nakashian, Chief Operating Officer, Communify Fincentric spoke with FinextraTV on the process behind product development. Discussing security and customer protection, Nakashian explains how events help to contextualise the importance of products and how the future will feature higher data personalisation and quality data for heightened scalability.

item.title: What’s next in wealth? Exploring European trends & priorities
item.description: How can wealth managers best serve the next generation of investors?

item.title: For Advisors, Succession Isn’t An Event - It’s a Strategy
item.description: I've worked with thousands of Financial Advisors and their firms across North America, and successio...

item.title: Quantum Machine Learning and Portfolio Optimization: The Next Frontier of Smart Investing
item.description: Introduction: The Portfolio Optimization Process Needs to Be Revamped. For decades, portfolio optimi...

item.title: AI Readiness for Financial Services: Take the Leap with Confidence
item.description: AI Readiness for Financial Services: Take the Leap with Confidence In 2025, its clear that AI adopti...

item.title: Sponsor's message: [New Report] Cross-Border Payments as the Next Fintech Frontier
item.description: [New Report] Cross-Border Payments as the Next Fintech Frontier
